tldr: Baptiste Veistroffer won the punchy 191.4 km Stage 2 to the Yitti Hills from a small group, beating Henok Mulubrhan and Thibaud Gruel and moving into the overall lead with the GC contenders still tightly bunched.

narrative.headline: Veistroffer's breakthrough at Yitti Hills
narrative.body: The 191.4 km second stage to the Yitti Hills was the first punchy test of the week. A small group went clear on the rolling finale, and Baptiste Veistroffer of Lotto-Intermarché proved strongest, taking the win 17 seconds ahead of Henok Mulubrhan and Thibaud Gruel. It was a breakthrough early-season result for the young Frenchman, who took over the leader's jersey.

gcImpact.headline: Veistroffer takes the overall lead
gcImpact.body: Veistroffer leads the GC after Stage 2, with Mulubrhan +25s and Gruel +27s. The pure climbers remained bunched behind, all waiting for the Stage 3 uphill and the Green Mountain finale to make their move.
